evidence_quotes.quote: not being able to use a product, in this case, a videogame, after I paid a full price for it.

paragraphs.text: Hi, I'd like to say Thank You for having this space that we have been given to express our concerns. Well I'd like express my concern as a consumer about not being able to use a product, in this case, a videogame, after I paid a full price for it. I don't think that limiting a videogame's life is something that consumers support specially since in the past we had not such issues, however I feel very mistreated as a consumer when a product of this kind has a limited lifetime, I understand that not everything last forever but I really feel like the industry of media should have a certain compromise to offer a product that will not disappear in a matter of years or even months as we saw happening lately in videogames like Concord, Babylon's Fall or The Crew, just to name a few. I was affected when The Crew ended it's lifetime and I felt that was not only unfair but also as a consumer felt mistreated since I had no choice but to lose access to something I paid for, that lead to not trust companies, now being careful or even investigate that the product I was buying had secured it's future. I honestly don't think that, as a consumer, I have to go thorough a research in order to make sure that the videogame I'm acquiring is not going to disappear, because I feel that I no longer have rights, that are, as simple as keeping something I paid money for, and is not a consumable, as in this case is digital media. Thank You
